chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: DOM stands for Document Object Model and it represents the HTML document as a tree of objects.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The DOM allows JavaScript to access and change all the elements of an HTML document.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The document object is the entry point to the DOM.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: getElementById selects an element by its id attribute.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: getElementsByClassName selects all elements with a given class name.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: getElementsByTagName selects all elements with a given tag name.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: querySelector selects the first element that matches a CSS selector.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: querySelectorAll selects all elements that match a CSS selector.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: querySelector and querySelectorAll are more modern and flexible than the older methods.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The innerHTML property gets or sets the HTML content of an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The textContent property gets or sets the text content of an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The innerText property is similar to textContent but is aware of styling.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The style property allows you to change the inline CSS styles of an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The classList property provides methods to add, remove, and toggle CSS classes.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: classList.add adds a class, classList.remove removes a class, classList.toggle toggles a class.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The setAttribute method sets the value of an attribute on an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The getAttribute method returns the value of a specified attribute.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: createElement is used to create a new HTML element dynamically.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: appendChild adds a node as the last child of a parent node.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: removeChild removes a child node from the DOM.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The parentElement property returns the parent element of the specified element.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: The children property returns a collection of child elements.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: DOM manipulation is the foundation of making websites interactive with JavaScript.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: Understanding the DOM is essential for any frontend developer.

chunks.title: JavaScript DOM Manipulation
chunks.text: In the next video, we will learn about JavaScript events which work closely with the DOM.

text: DOM stands for Document Object Model and it represents the HTML document as a tree of objects. The DOM allows JavaScript to access and change all the elements of an HTML document. The document object is the entry point to the DOM. getElementById selects an element by its id attribute. getElementsByClassName selects all elements with a given class name. getElementsByTagName selects all elements with a given tag name. querySelector selects the first element that matches a CSS selector. querySelectorAll selects all elements that match a CSS selector. querySelector and querySelectorAll are more modern and flexible than the older methods. The innerHTML property gets or sets the HTML content of an element. The textContent property gets or sets the text content of an element. The innerText property is similar to textContent but is aware of styling. The style property allows you to change the inline CSS styles of an element. The classList property provides methods to add, remove, and toggle CSS classes. classList.add adds a class, classList.remove removes a class, classList.toggle toggles a class. The setAttribute method sets the value of an attribute on an element. The getAttribute method returns the value of a specified attribute. createElement is used to create a new HTML element dynamically. appendChild adds a node as the last child of a parent node. removeChild removes a child node from the DOM. The parentElement property returns the parent element of the specified element. The children property returns a collection of child elements. DOM manipulation is the foundation of making websites interactive with JavaScript. Understanding the DOM is essential for any frontend developer. In the next video, we will learn about JavaScript events which work closely with the DOM.
